div1.entryFree: Maim, vb. to lame, to cripple: you --ed the jurisdiction of all bishops, H8 III, 2, 312. with such --ed rites, Hml. V, 1, 242 (defective). a judgment --ed and most imperfect, Oth. I, 3, 99. I am --ed for ever, V, 1, 27. == to hurt in general: did win whom he would m. Pilgr. 312 (in the rhyme). it --ed you two outright, Shr. V, 2, 62. Cade says mained for maimed, H6B IV, 2, 172.
